channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: H-Probes: Extracting Hierarchical Structures From Latent Representations of Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.00847v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representing and navigating hierarchy is a fundamental primitive of reasoning. Large language models have demonstrated proficiency in a wide variety of tasks requiring hierarchical reasoning, but there exists limited analysis on how the models geometr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, analysis, reasoning, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Model Organisms Are Leaky: Perplexity Differencing Often Reveals Finetuning Objectives
item.description: arXiv:2605.00994v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Finetuning can significantly modify the behavior of large language models, including introducing harmful or unsafe behaviors. To study these risks, researchers develop model organisms: models finetuned to exhibit specific known behaviors for controlle...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, RAG, API, experiment, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can AI Debias the News? LLM Interventions Improve Cross-Partisan Receptivity but LLMs Overestimate Their Own Effectiveness
item.description: arXiv:2605.01006v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Partisan news media erode cross-partisan trust, but large language models (LLMs) offer a potential means of debiasing such content at scale. Across two pre-registered experiments, we tested whether LLM-generated debiasing of liberal news headlines cou...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, study, arxiv, large language model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CLEAR: Revealing How Noise and Ambiguity Degrade Reliability in LLMs for Medicine
item.description: arXiv:2605.01011v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical large language model (LLM) evaluations rely on simplified, exam-style benchmarks that rarely reflect the ambiguity of real-world medical inquiries. We introduce the CLinical Evaluation of Ambiguity and Reliability (CLEAR) framework, which asse...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, framework, arxiv, large language model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Psychologically Potent, Computationally Invisible: LLMs Generate Social-Comparison Triggers They Fail to Detect
item.description: arXiv:2605.01017v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Xiaohongshu Social Comparison Reader Elicitation (XHS-SCoRE), a reader-grounded benchmark for detecting if a text-only Xiaohongshu (RedNote) post elicits UPWARD, DOWNWARD, or NEUTRAL/no clear social comparison from a first-person reader p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, prompt, study, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Theoretical Game of Attacks via Compositional Skills
item.description: arXiv:2605.01034v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language models grow increasingly capable, concerns about their safe deployment have intensified. While numerous alignment strategies aim to restrict harmful behavior, these defenses can still be circumvented through carefully designed advers...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, prompt, prompting, framework, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Systematic Exploration of Text Decomposition and Budget Distribution in Differentially Private Text Obfuscation
item.description: arXiv:2605.01065v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The goal of differentially private text obfuscation is to obfuscate, or "perturb", input texts with Differential Privacy (DP) guarantees, such that the private output texts are quantifiably indistinguishable from the originals. While perturbation at t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, reasoning, experiment, research, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Controlled Paraphrase Geometry in Sentence Embedding Space: Local Manifold Modeling and Latent Probing
item.description: arXiv:2605.01073v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The paper studies the local geometry of embedding clouds induced by \emph{controlled local classes of semantically close sentences}. The central question is how controlled paraphrase-like semantic variation is organized in sentence embedding space and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, embedding, template, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TADI: Tool-Augmented Drilling Intelligence via Agentic LLM Orchestration over Heterogeneous Wellsite Data
item.description: arXiv:2605.00060v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present TADI (Tool-Augmented Drilling Intelligence), an agentic AI system that transforms drilling operational data into evidence-based analytical intelligence. Applied to the Equinor Volve Field dataset, TADI integrates 1,759 daily drilling report...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, API, model, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentReputation: A Decentralized Agentic AI Reputation Framework
item.description: arXiv:2605.00073v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decentralized, agentic AI marketplaces are rapidly emerging to support software engineering tasks such as debugging, patch generation, and security auditing, often operating without centralized oversight. However, existing reputation mechanisms fail i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, RAG, API, platform, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Minimal, Local, Causal Explanations for Jailbreak Success in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.00123v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safety trained large language models (LLMs) can often be induced to answer harmful requests through jailbreak prompts. Because we lack a robust understanding of why LLMs are susceptible to jailbreaks, future frontier models operating more autonomously...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, release, prompt, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TUR-DPO: Topology- and Uncertainty-Aware Direct Preference Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.00224v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Aligning large language models (LLMs) with human preferences is commonly done via reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) with Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) or, more simply, via Direct Preference Optimization (DPO). While DPO is stable ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, RAG, reasoning, RLHF, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ARMOR 2025: A Military-Aligned Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Model Safety Beyond Civilian Contexts
item.description: arXiv:2605.00245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are now being explored for defense applications that require reliable and legally compliant decision support. They also hold significant potential to enhance decision making, coordination, and operational efficiency in mil...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt, framework, alignment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Token Arena: A Continuous Benchmark Unifying Energy and Cognition in AI Inference
item.description: arXiv:2605.00300v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Public inference benchmarks compare AI systems at the model and provider level, but the unit at which deployment decisions are actually made is the endpoint: the (provider, model, stock-keeping-unit) tuple at which a specific quantization, decoding st...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, reasoning, release, retrieval, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentFloor: How Far Up the tool use Ladder Can Small Open-Weight Models Go?
item.description: arXiv:2605.00334v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Production agentic systems make many model calls per user request, and most of those calls are short, structured, and routine. This raises a practical routing question that existing evaluations do not directly answer: which parts of an agent workflow ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, release, GPT, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Agentopic: A Generative AI Agent Workflow for Explainable Topic Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2605.00833v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentopic is a novel agent-based workflow for explainable topic modeling that leverages the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). Existing topic modeling approaches such as Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) and BERTopic often lack tr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, RAG, reasoning, embedding, GPT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Polynomial-Time Optimal Group Selection via the Double-Commutator Eigenvalue Problem
item.description: arXiv:2605.00834v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The algebraic diversity framework replaces temporal averaging over multiple observations with algebraic group action on a single observation for second-order statistical estimation. The central open problem in this framework is $\textit{group selectio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, RAG, framework, arxiv, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GAZE: Grounded Agentic Zero-shot Evaluation with Viewer-Level Tools and Literature Retrieval on Rare Brain MRI
item.description: arXiv:2605.00876v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) read an image and produce text in a single forward pass, whereas radiologists typically inspect an image several times and consult the literature before writing a report. We introduce GAZE (Grounded Agentic Zero-shot Eval...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, image, RAG, library, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: StyleShield: Exposing the Fragility of AIGC Detectors through Continuous Controllable Style Transfer
item.description: arXiv:2605.00924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-generated content (AIGC) detectors are increasingly deployed in high-stakes settings such as academic integrity screening, yet their reliability rests on a fundamental paradox: as language models are trained on human-written corpora, the statistica...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, RAG, embedding, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Linking spatial biology and clinical histology via Haiku
item.description: arXiv:2605.00925v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Integrating molecular, morphological, and clinical data is essential for basic and translational biomedical research, yet systematic frameworks for jointly modeling these modalities remain limited. Here we present Haiku, a tri-modal contrastive learni...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, context, analysis, embedding, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PhaseNet++: Phase-Aware Frequency-Domain Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control Systems via Phase Coherence Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2605.00929v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multivariate time series anomaly detection in ICS has attracted growing attention due to the increasing threat of cyber-physical attacks on critical infrastructure. State-of-the-art methods model inter-sensor relationships from raw time-domain amplitu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, attention, study, transformer, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, prompt engineering, context window, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, reasoning, knowledge base, retrieval, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.
item.description: SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, API, retrieval, augmented, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: DIAGRAMS: A Review Framework for Reasoning-Level Attribution in Diagram QA
item.description: arXiv:2605.00905v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diagram question answering (Diagram QA) requires reasoning-level attribution that links each question-answer pair to all visual regions needed to derive the answer, rather than only the region containing the final response. Creating such structured ev...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, RAG, reasoning, release, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Compared to What? Baselines and Metrics for Counterfactual Prompting
item.description: arXiv:2605.01048v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Counterfactual prompting (i.e., perturbing a single factor and measuring output change) is widely used to evaluate things like LLM bias and CoT faithfulness. But in this work we argue that observed effects cannot be attributed to the targeted factor w...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, prompt, prompting, framework, CoT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Are Tools All We Need? Unveiling the Tool-Use Tax in LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.00136v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-augmented reasoning has become a popular direction for LLM-based agents, and it is widely assumed to improve reasoning and reliability. However, we demonstrate that this consensus does not always hold: in the presence of semantic distractors, too...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, reasoning, prompt, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Agentic AI for Trip Planning Optimization Application
item.description: arXiv:2605.00276v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Trip planning for intelligent vehicles increasingly requires selecting optimal routes rather than merely producing feasible itineraries, as interacting factors such as travel time, energy consumption, and traffic conditions directly affect plan qualit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, reasoning, ICL, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sparse Regression under Correlation and Weak Signals: A Reproducible Benchmark of Classical and Bayesian Methods
item.description: arXiv:2605.00835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Choosing between classical and Bayesian sparse regression methods involves a real trade-off: penalized estimators like Lasso run in milliseconds but give no uncertainty estimates,while Horseshoe and Spike-and-Slab priors produce full posteriors but ne...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: fastapi_mcp - Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> chain-of-thought, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: Fast Log-Domain Sinkhorn Optimal Transport with Warp-Level GPU Reductions
item.description: arXiv:2605.00837v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Entropic regularized optimal transport (OT) via the Sinkhorn algorithm has become a fundamental tool in machine learning, yet existing implementations either suffer from numerical instability for small regularization parameters or incur significant ov...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, library, analysis, memory, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, step-by-step, prompt, example, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Causal Foundations of Collective Agency
item.description: arXiv:2605.00248v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A key challenge for the safety of advanced AI systems is the possibility that multiple simpler agents might inadvertently form a collective agent with capabilities and goals distinct from those of any individual. More generally, determining when a gro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, framework, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Euler to Dormand-Prince: ODE Solvers for Flow Matching Generative Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.00836v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sampling from Flow Matching generative models requires solving an ordinary differential equation (ODE) whose computational cost is dominated by neural network forward passes. We derive four classical ODE solvers -- Euler, Explicit Midpoint, Classical ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, ICL, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Review of the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve and a Proof About the Area Beneath It
item.description: arXiv:2605.00926v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curve of a binary classifier has often been utilized to measure the performance of the classifier. The area beneath this curve is used in particular because of its quoted probabilistic interpretation as bein...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
